revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Izushi Shrine]][[fr:Izushi-jinja]][[ja:出石神社]][[ko:이즈시 신사]][[zh:出石神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izushi Jinja
出石神社 | image = File:Izushi-jinja shinmon.JPG | caption = Entry of Izushi Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.481944|N|134.870278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amenohiboko|qid=Q713100|1=Amenohiboko}}; {{ill|Izushiyamae no okami|qid=Q112298467|1=Izushiyamae no okami}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 99 Miyauchi, Izushicho, Toyooka-shi, Hyōgo-ken | website = | architecture_style = | festival = October 20 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}}} | prefecture= {{ill|Toyooka|qid=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}} }} {{nihongo|'''Izushi Jinja'''|出石神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Izushi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|qid=Q696388|lt=Toyooka|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}} in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|qid=Q1053727|lt=Tajima Province|1=Tajima Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 20.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Izushi Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Izushiyamae-Ōkami|ja|伊豆志八前大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushiyamae-Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''|伊豆志八前大神|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Amenohiboko]]'''|天日槍命|}} ==History== The Izushi Shrine is located at the foot of a mountain on the eastern edge of the Izushi basin in the northern part of Hyōgo prefecture. It is located about 2 kilometers north of the current Izushi city area.The Ishrine is the center of the legend of Prince Amenohiboko of {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, which is recorded in the "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki]]", who settled in this area during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. He brought with him eight sacred treasures from Silla, including a sacred spear, and married a daughter of the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of Tajima. The origins of Izushi Jinja are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, it is believed that it began as the family shrine of the descendants of the legendary prince Amenohiboko; however, there is some confusion as to whether the object of worship is the spirit of the prince, or the spirits of the magical objects brought from Silla. The shrine first appears in the historical documents in tax records for Tajima Province dated 737. It appears thereafter in the various historical chronicles in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, including the ''[[Engishiki]]'' and the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' and the ''{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|qid=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}''. The ''Engishiki'' lists it as a {{nihongo|Myōjin Taisha|名神大社||}} which holds eight ''kami'', and as the ''ichinomiya'' of the province. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the main castle of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|Yamana clan|qid=Q2975406|lt=Yamana clan|1=Yamana clan}} was located to the north of this shrine. The shrine prospered from this proximity until 1504, when it was burned down during an internal conflict within the Yamana clan. It was rebuilt by 1525; however, when {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} occupied the area in 1580, he confiscated the territory of the Yamana clan, and the shrine fell into decline. Under the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]], the area became {{ill|Izushi Domain|en|Izushi Domain|qid=Q10863249|lt=Izushi Domain|1=Izushi Domain}}, ruled by the [[Koide clan]] followed by the {{ill|Sengoku clan|en|Sengoku clan|qid=Q11378184|lt=Sengoku clan|1=Sengoku clan}}.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 2nd Rank |国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei-chūsha|Kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. However, the shrine burned down in 1910. The current buildings date from the 1914 reconstruction and are a Tangible Cultural Property of Toyooka city. The precincts are still very large, with an area of 22,000 square meters, of which a 1000 square meter area in the northeastern corner is fenced off with access prohibited. In Edo period maps, this area is marked "Amenohiboko Mausoleum". In the Torii neighborhood, about 500 meters west of the precincts, the remnants of an old ''[[torii]]'' gate were excavated along with many old coins in 1933, indicating that the precincts were once much larger. The excavated ''torii'' remnants have been designated as a Tangible Cultural Property of Toyooka City{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Izushi-jinja shaden.JPG|Precincts File:Izushi-jinja honden.JPG|Honden File:Izushi-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Izushi-jinja kinsokuchi.JPG|"Forbidden area" File:Izushi-jinja ichi-no-torii.JPG|FIrst Torii ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *''[[Wakizashi]]'' short sword, {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, 39.2 centimeters, inscribed "Tanshū Kunimitsu" and with eight {{ill|Siddhaṃ script|en|Siddhaṃ script|qid=Q250379|lt=Siddhaṃ script|1=Siddhaṃ script}} letters.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6286|title=脇指〈銘但州住国光（外ニ梵字アリ）|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Izushi-jinja}} *[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/spot/result/781 Hyōgo Tourist Information] *[https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6323031.html Hyōgo Jinjacho home page] ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Izushi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Izushi-jinja}} [[fr:Izushi-jinja]] [[ja:出石神社]] [[ko:이즈시 신사]] [[zh:出石神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3156511}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajima Province]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
